judge_summary: Upstream verdict pass was strong — all 6 drafts kept as-is with reasonable tier calls. The notable miss was obra/superpowers, promoted along with Antigravity 2.0 and Graphiti 0.29.1. Briefing now reads as a security-led week, a major Google I/O frontier-model beat, and local-tooling momentum.

verdicts.what: Security release that closes an SSRF bypass via IPv6-encoded URL forms.
verdicts.why_it_matters: Directly relevant to production agents that fetch external references; this closes a real exploit path in our stack.
verdicts.adoption_cost: 1 hour to bump package and rerun integration tests.
verdicts.next_action: Patch this sprint and validate with SSRF regression tests.
verdicts.why_this_week: Security advisory published with a confirmed exploit pattern.

verdicts.what: Agentic skills framework and software methodology for coding workflows.
verdicts.why_it_matters: Already adjacent to our internal workflow, and recent traction suggests better reusable patterns for agent guidance.
verdicts.adoption_cost: 4 hours to trial one skill path in a sandbox branch.
verdicts.next_action: Run one lab and compare execution quality against baseline prompts.

verdicts.what: Open-weights frontier model with strong benchmarks but unresolved compliance posture.
verdicts.why_it_matters: Team demand is high, so we need an explicit do-not-adopt signal to avoid accidental policy drift.
verdicts.next_action: Hold and re-evaluate only if legal and security sign off.
